prompt: Task: Generate a unified diff (delta) to improve the baseline CIFAR-10 model.
This stronger CIFAR-10 model is a reference for the kind of improvement pattern to imitate, not a file to copy line-by-line.
1. READ the target baseline code structure carefully (class names, layer names, method signatures)
2. READ the source model and infer the improvement pattern that likely makes it more reliable on CIFAR-10
3. Transfer the pattern to the target baseline while MATCHING the target baseline's actual structure
4. Use the EXACT variable names from the target baseline
5. Use REAL line numbers and real context lines from the target baseline code above
Generate exactly 3 XML tags and nothing else:
+<new line that keeps the baseline structure valid>
- Patch only the baseline Python code shown inside <nn>
- Treat the source model as an analogy only; never copy its line numbers or class names unless they already exist in the target baseline
- Do not patch hp.json or transform files inside the diff
- Do not include <hp>, <tr>, <nn>, JSON, or transform code inside the diff
- Keep supported_hyperparameters(), train_setup(self, prm), and learn(self, train_data) valid
- If you add self.X in forward(), you MUST add self.X in __init__()
- Keep CIFAR-10 shapes safe; prefer AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1)) before the classifier when helpful
